title: Weather Update: मॉनसून की आहट के बीच 17 राज्यों में भारी बारिश का अलर्ट, 70 KM/H की रफ्तार से चलेंगी हवाएं - hindi.oneworldnews.com
article_text:
Weather Update Today: देशभर में मौसम का मिजाज तेजी से बदल रहा है। जहां एक ओर उत्तर-पश्चिम, पश्चिम और मध्य भारत के कई राज्य भीषण गर्मी और हीटवेव की चपेट में हैं, वहीं दूसरी तरफ दक्षिण और पूर्वोत्तर भारत में बारिश और आंधी की गतिविधियां बढ़ गई हैं। भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) ने हिमाचल प्रदेश, पंजाब, असम, नागालैंड समेत 17 राज्यों में भारी बारिश और तेज हवाओं का अलर्ट जारी किया है। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार इन राज्यों में 40 से 70 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं। इसके अलावा अगले 48 घंटों में दक्षिण-पश्चिम मॉनसून अंडमान सागर और दक्षिण बंगाल की खाड़ी के कुछ हिस्सों में प्रवेश कर सकता है।
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार 16 मई के आसपास दक्षिण-पश्चिम मॉनसून अंडमान सागर और दक्षिण बंगाल की खाड़ी में आगे बढ़ सकता है। मौसम विशेषज्ञों का मानना है कि मॉनसून की शुरुआती हलचल के कारण कई राज्यों में मौसम तेजी से बदल सकता है। हालांकि, पश्चिमी और उत्तर-पश्चिमी भारत के कई इलाकों में गर्मी का प्रकोप अभी जारी रहेगा। खासतौर पर राजस्थान में लोगों को अगले कुछ दिनों तक भीषण लू का सामना करना पड़ सकता है।
दिल्ली-एनसीआर में हाल ही में आंधी और ओलावृष्टि के बाद मौसम थोड़ा बदला है। IMD के मुताबिक 14 मई को हल्की बारिश या बूंदाबांदी हो सकती है। हालांकि आने वाले दो दिनों में तापमान दोबारा 41°C तक पहुंच सकता है।
उत्तर प्रदेश में 14 मई तक मौसम में बदलाव देखने को मिल सकता है। कई जिलों में हल्की से मध्यम बारिश के साथ तेज हवाएं चलने की संभावना है।
इस दौरान 30 से 60 KM/H की रफ्तार से हवाएं चल सकती हैं।
Read More: Weather Update: भारत में समय से पहले मॉनसून की एंट्री, 16 मई से भारी बारिश और तेज हवाओं का अलर्ट
मौसम विभाग ने बिहार में 14 से 17 मई तक आंधी-बारिश का अलर्ट जारी किया है। कई इलाकों में 50 से 70 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं।
हिमाचल प्रदेश में 15 और 16 मई को मौसम खराब रह सकता है। कई ऊंचाई वाले क्षेत्रों में बारिश और बर्फबारी की संभावना है।
जहां देश के कई हिस्सों में बारिश हो रही है, वहीं राजस्थान के कई जिलों में भीषण गर्मी और लू का असर जारी है। IMD ने पश्चिमी राजस्थान में 15 मई तक हीटवेव अलर्ट जारी किया है।
कुछ इलाकों में धूलभरी आंधी भी चल सकती है।
दक्षिण बंगाल की खाड़ी, अंडमान सागर और अंडमान एवं निकोबार द्वीप समूह के कुछ हिस्सों में के 16 मई 2026 के आसपास दक्षिण-पश्चिम मानसून के आगे बढ़ने के लिए परिस्थितियाँ अनुकूल हो रही हैं।
उत्तर-पश्चिम, पश्चिम और मध्य भारत के कुछ हिस्सों में कई दिनों… pic.twitter.com/Exiqy5G2gb
— India Meteorological Department (@Indiametdept) May 13, 2026
पंजाब में अगले 24 घंटे के दौरान हल्की बारिश और तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं। अमृतसर, लुधियाना, जालंधर और मोहाली समेत कई जिलों में मौसम बदला हुआ रहेगा।
We’re now on WhatsApp. Click to join.

Five-day weather warning issued; thunderstorms, gusty winds likely in Andhra till May 17
Five-day weather warning issued; thunderstorms, gusty winds likely in Andhra till May 17
Southwest monsoon likely to hit Bay of Bengal, Andaman by May 16
By -  Newsmeter Network
Published on : 14 May 2026 10:21 AM

Amaravati : Meteorological Centre in Amaravati said the southwest monsoon may hit the south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea, and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands by May 16.

The centre said the conditions are turning favourable for the early southwest monsoon over parts of the south Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea, and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands.

According to the weather update issued, a low-pressure area over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal has intensified into a well-marked low-pressure area. As of 8:30 am IST, the system persisted over the same region with an associated cyclonic circulation extending up to 5.8 km above mean sea level.

The department also noted that a north-south trough extends from southwest Madhya Pradesh to south interior Karnataka, passing through Madhya Maharashtra and north interior Karnataka up to 1.5 km above mean sea level.

Thunderstorms, Lightning, and Gusty Winds Likely Across Andhra:

The Meteorological Centre in Amaravati has issued a five-day weather warning for Andhra Pradesh and Yanam, forecasting thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds across several regions till May 17.

On May 13, gusty winds with speeds of 30-40 kmph are expected at isolated places over Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Yanam and Rayalaseema.

From May 14 to May 17, wind speeds are likely to intensify, reaching 40-50 kmph at isolated locations across the same regions.

The department said there will be no large change in maximum temperatures over Andhra Pradesh and Yanam during the next five days.

title: NFR installing Automatic Weather Stations to strengthen monsoon safety - MorungExpress
article_text:
NORTH-EAST
  13th May 2026

Maligaon, May 13 (MExN): In a major initiative aimed at strengthening railway safety and disaster preparedness during the monsoon season, the Northeast Frontier Railway (NFR) has begun installing Automatic Weather Stations (AWS) at vulnerable locations across its network to enable accurate and real-time weather forecasting.

According to an official statement, a total of nine Automatic Weather Stations are being set up at strategically identified locations under the NFR zone. The selected sites include the Lumding–Badarpur section in Assam, the Katakhal–Sairang section connecting Mizoram, and the Jiribam–Khongsang section in Manipur.

These regions are particularly prone to heavy rainfall, flooding and landslides during the rainy season, making continuous weather monitoring crucial for safe train operations.

So far, three Automatic Weather Stations have been successfully installed, while the remaining six are under various stages of installation and are expected to be completed by the end of May 2026.

The installation work is being carried out in coordination with the India Meteorological Department (IMD). The AWS network will provide precise and location-specific weather data, enabling railway authorities to take timely and informed decisions during emergencies and adverse weather conditions.

The initiative is expected to significantly enhance preparedness and response mechanisms, especially in flood-prone and landslide-sensitive sections of the railway network. 

The Automatic Weather Stations are equipped with advanced sensors capable of monitoring multiple weather parameters with high accuracy. The rain gauge sensors can record rainfall intensity of up to 900 mm per hour, while temperature sensors can measure temperatures ranging from minus 40 degrees Celsius to plus 75 degrees Celsius.

Wind monitoring sensors can record wind speeds of up to 80 metres per second. In addition, the stations are fitted with humidity sensors covering a range from 0 to 100 per cent and pressure sensors capable of measuring atmospheric pressure up to 1200 hPa.

title: Storm causes damage to 168 structures in Mizoram - The News Mill
article_text:
A spell of intense pre-monsoon weather has battered Mizoram over the past three days, damaging property and infrastructure across multiple districts, officials said on Wednesday. No loss of life has been reported.

Discover more weather conditions Newspapers weather

According to the State Emergency Operation Centre under the Disaster Management and Rehabilitation department, at least 168 structures have been destroyed or partially damaged since Monday. These include 155 private buildings and 13 government, community and church establishments.

Lunglei district was the worst affected, with 85 private houses damaged or flattened. Three Baptist church buildings also sustained significant damage.

In Aizawl district, 44 houses were damaged, while 14 homes were destroyed in Siaha district, which borders Myanmar. In total, eight districts reported damage.

Services hit, connectivity disrupted

Discover more Weather Local News political

High-velocity winds uprooted electric poles in several locations, leading to power outages, including in parts of the state capital. Road connectivity was disrupted by landslides and rockfalls, making several stretches inaccessible.

Mobile and internet services were also affected due to technical faults triggered by the weather conditions, hampering communication in some areas.

Relief operations underway

District administrations, assisted by the State Disaster Response Force, police and local volunteers, have been mobilised for relief and restoration work.

Officials said immediate assistance includes financial aid, tarpaulins for temporary shelter, and rehabilitation support for families whose homes were destroyed. Damage assessment is ongoing, with authorities urging residents to remain alert amid continuing pre-monsoon weather conditions.

title: Monsoon 2026: Here's IMD's latest prediction on arrival dates for Kerala to Delhi - MSN
article_text:
As large parts of India continue to reel under intense summer heat, attention is now shifting to the arrival of the southwest monsoon — the weather system that delivers nearly 70% of the country’s annual rainfall and plays a crucial role in agriculture, water supply and power demand.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the southwest monsoon is expected to reach the Kerala coast around its normal onset date in early June, even as some global weather models hint at a slightly early arrival this year.
Must read | From food prices to power supply: Why monsoon still decides the fate of India’s economy
IMD Director General Mrutyunjay Mohapatra said monsoon rains are likely to begin over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands between May 14 and May 20 — an important early milestone in the monsoon’s journey toward mainland India.
What is the usual monsoon timeline?
Under normal conditions, the southwest monsoon follows a fairly predictable progression across the country:
The IMD notes that the monsoon typically advances northward in phases or “surges”, influenced by sea surface temperatures, wind patterns and low-pressure systems over the Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea.
Is monsoon arriving early this year?
While the IMD has not yet officially declared an early onset, several international weather models, including the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF), suggest the monsoon may reach Kerala by the last week of May.
Don't miss | BT Explainer: Why scientists fear weakening Atlantic Ocean currents could alter Indian monsoon
Some forecasts indicate that monsoon currents could reach the Andaman region as early as May 18 and advance rapidly toward southern India if atmospheric conditions remain favourable.
However, weather experts caution that an early onset over Kerala does not always mean the monsoon will advance quickly across the entire country. In some years, progress slows after the initial onset phase due to changing wind conditions and rainfall patterns.
IMD predicts below-normal seasonal rainfall
In its first long-range forecast issued in April, the IMD predicted that India is likely to receive below-normal monsoon rainfall during the June-September season. Rainfall is estimated at 92% of the Long Period Average (LPA), with the possibility of El Niño conditions developing later in the monsoon season.
The weather agency said neutral conditions currently prevail in the Pacific Ocean, but climate models indicate El Niño could emerge by July, potentially affecting rainfall distribution across India.
Must read | Inside India’s agricultural matrix: How the monsoon shapes farming cycle & crop yields
At the same time, the IMD expects pre-monsoon rainfall activity in May to remain normal to above normal across many regions, offering some relief from ongoing heatwave conditions.
The southwest monsoon is critical for India’s economy, especially the farm sector. A large part of India’s kharif crop cultivation depends on timely and well-distributed rainfall. Reservoir levels, hydropower generation and rural consumption are also closely linked to monsoon performance.
Discover the powerful, protective, and sometimes ruthless world of female animals (Source: Freepik)
When we think of powerful predators, we often picture males on the hunt. But in the animal world, it’s often the females who rule — not just with strength, but with strategy, survival instincts, and sometimes even deadly force.
From tiny mosquitoes to big cats, here’s a look at some of Earth's most formidable female animals.
They may seem harmless, but female mosquitoes are responsible for more deaths than any other creature.
Unlike males, females bite humans because they need blood to develop their eggs. In the process, they spread serious diseases like malaria, dengue, and Zika — killing hundreds of thousands of people every year.

title: At least 89 dead as freak summer hailstorm and heavy rains strike northern India - The Independent
article_text:
Asia
India
At least 89 dead as freak summer hailstorm and heavy rains strike northern India

Uttar Pradesh state is particularly vulnerable to violent pre-monsoon storms but they are unusual in mid-May

Stuti Mishra
Thursday 14 May 2026 07:16 BST
Copy link
Bookmark
Comments
Go to comments
Bookmark popover

Removed from bookmarks

Close popover
open image in gallery
File. Dark clouds loom over the Ganga river in Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh (AFP via Getty)
Your support helps us to tell the story
SUPPORT NOW

From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging.

At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground. Your donation allows us to keep sending journalists to speak to both sides of the story.

The Independent is trusted by Americans across the entire political spectrum. And unlike many other quality news outlets, we choose not to lock Americans out of our reporting and analysis with paywalls. We believe quality journalism should be available to everyone, paid for by those who can afford it.

Your support makes all the difference.
Read more

Nearly 90 people were killed after an unseasonal violent storm hit the northern Indian state of Uttar Pradesh with heavy rain, hail and lightning on Wednesday.

The storm swept through five districts, uprooting trees and billboards and sending them crashing into cars. It also disrupted mobile networks in many areas, hampering rescue and relief work and making it difficult for officials to do a full assessment of the damage.

“About 89 people were killed because of the storm, lightning and rain-related incidents," an unnamed state official told Reuters, adding that many of the deaths were caused by falling trees and collapsing walls of homes.

In Bhadohi district, which was hit the hardest, at least 16 people died in separate incidents and six were injured, two of them seriously, local police said.

In Fatehpur, nine people were killed and 16 injured. Additional district magistrate Avinash Tripathi said eight people, including five women, died in Khaga tehsil and the ninth victim, a woman, was killed when a house wall collapsed in Sadar tehsil.

In Budaun, five people were killed and as many injured. The dead included two young girls. Mausami, 10, and Rajni, 9, died after the mud wall of a hut they had taken shelter in collapsed. Two women who were with them in the hut were seriously injured.

open image in gallery
File. People take shelter outside a gift shop after a hailstorm in Amritsar (AFP via Getty)

In another incident in Tark Paroli village, a woman named Laxmi, 40, died after a tree fell on a tubewell room where several people had taken shelter, bringing down the roof.

Three people were injured, including a woman whose condition was described as critical. A truck driver died after an eucalyptus tree fell on his vehicle near Bisoli and a 22-year-old died after a tree fell on him as he was on his way to deliver food to his father.

In Sonbhadra, a man died after a tree fell on him when he stopped to shelter under it. In Chandauli, two people died in separate incidents of wall collapse and a tree falling.

Chief minister Adityanath directed district officials to reach affected areas and complete relief work within 24 hours, warning that negligence would not be tolerated. He also ordered compensation for losses and directed revenue and agriculture departments, alongside insurance companies, to survey damage and report to the government.

Uttar Pradesh, which at 240 million is home to more people than Brazil, is particularly vulnerable to violent pre-monsoon storms.

However, hailstorms of this intensity are unusual in mid-May, when the state is usually in the grip of peak summer heat, with temperatures often exceeding 40C and the monsoon still weeks away.

India has been enduring an intense heatwave this summer. One day in late April, the South Asian nation accounted for all of the world’s 50 hottest cities.

title: Nearly 90 killed as severe storm hits India’s Uttar Pradesh state - PHOTO - Caliber.Az
article_text:
WORLD
A+
A-
Nearly 90 killed as severe storm hits India’s Uttar Pradesh state PHOTO
14 May 2026 10:44

Nearly 90 people have been killed after a severe storm system struck India’s most populous state, Uttar Pradesh, according to the state disaster management and relief office.

In a statement on X, the state’s relief commissioner said 89 deaths were confirmed on May 13 due to “adverse weather conditions,” including heavy rain, hail, and lightning, Caliber.Az reports, citing Reuters.

The storms also left 53 people injured, damaged 87 homes, and killed 114 livestock, according to the same statement.

Storms are a frequent occurrence in northern India during the pre-monsoon season from March to June, before the arrival of the annual monsoon brings relief from extreme heat.

Television footage showed strong winds uprooting trees and billboards, some of which fell onto vehicles, while dust storms scattered debris and damaged roadside stalls.

Some of the deaths were caused by falling trees and collapsing house walls.

Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has instructed officials to support survivors and distribute financial aid within 24 hours, according to authorities.

By Bakhtiyar Abbasov

Caliber.Az

title: Nearly 90 killed as storm lashes India's most populous state of Uttar Pradesh - RNZ
article_text:
WORLD
5:02 pm today
Nearly 90 killed as storm lashes India's most populous state of Uttar Pradesh
5:02 pm today 
Share this 
Share on Twitter
Share on Facebook
Share via email
Share on Reddit
Share on Linked In

Storms are common in the northern state during the hot season from March to June, before monsoon rains bring respite (file image). Photo: Debajyoti Chakraborty / NurPhoto via AFP

Almost 90 people were killed in a violent storm that pounded India's most populous state of Uttar Pradesh with rain and hail, a state official told Reuters.

Storms are common in the northern state during the hot season from March to June, before monsoon rains bring respite.

Television images showed trees and billboards uprooted by gusts of wind in Wednesday's storm, sending some crashing into cars amid clouds of dust and debris that blew down wooden furniture at roadside stalls.

"About 89 people were killed because of the storm, lightning, and rain-related incidents," said the official, who sought anonymity because of lack of authorisation to speak to the media.

Some deaths were caused by falling trees and the walls of homes, a state relief official told Reuters, without giving immediate details of rescue efforts.

Senior government officials did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

- Reuters

title: Tamil Nadu Weather Updates: நீலகிரிக்கு கனமழை அலெர்ட்... இன்று எங்கெல்லாம் மழைக்கு வாய்ப்பு... வானிலை தகவல் - Times Now Tamil
article_text:
Tamil Nadu Weather Updates:தமிழகத்தில் கடந்த 24 மணி நேரத்தில் நெல்லை, கன்னியாகுமரி ஆகிய மாவட்டங்களில் மழை பெய்துள்ளது. நேற்றிரவு தமிழகத்தின் பல்வேறு பகுதிகளில் மிதமான மழை பெய்துள்ளது. இந்நிலையில் தென்மேற்கு பருவமழை ஓரிரு தினங்களில் தொடங்கும் என வானிலை மையம் தெரிவித்துள்ளது. இதுதொடர்பாக சென்னை வானிலை ஆய்வு மையம் வெளியிட்டுள்ள செய்திக்குறிப்பில் கூறியிருப்பதாவது, “தென்மேற்கு பருவமழை தெற்கு வங்கக்கடலின் சில பகுதிகள், அந்தமான் கடல் பகுதிகள், அந்தமான் மற்றும் நிகோபார் தீவு பகுதிகளில் 16 ஆம் தேதி வாக்கில் துவங்கக்கூடும்.தென்மேற்கு வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் நிலவிய காற்றழுத்த தாழ்வு பகுதி, வடக்கு திசையில் நகர்ந்து, தென்மேற்கு வங்கக்கடல் மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய மத்தியமேற்கு வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் வலுப்பெற்று ஆழ்ந்த காற்றழுத்த தாழ்வு பகுதியாக நிலவுகிறது.

இதன்காரணமாக தமிழகத்தில் இன்று மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்கள் மற்றும் டெல்டா மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகத்தில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். நீலகிரி மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னல் மற்றும் பலத்த காற்றுடன் (மணிக்கு 30 முதல் 40 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்தில்) கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

இதையும் படிங்க: புதுச்சேரியில் இன்று மின் தடை அறிவிப்பு.. 5 மணி நேரம் பவர் கட்..!

தமிழகத்தில் நாளை தென் தமிழக கடலோர மாவட்டங்கள், மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்கள் மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகம், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.கோயம்புத்தூர் மாவட்டத்தின்மலைப்பகுதிகள் மற்றும் நீலகிரி மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிருஇடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

16-05-2026: தென் தமிழக கடலோர மாவட்டங்கள், மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்கள் மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகம், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.தேனி மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

17-05-2026: தென் தமிழகம் மற்றும் மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகம், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். நீலகிரி மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

18-05-2026: மேற்கு தொடர்ச்சி மலை மாவட்டங்கள் மற்றும் வடக்கு உள் தமிழக மாவட்டங்களில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும். ஏனைய தமிழகம், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளில் லேசான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.நீலகிரி, தேனி மற்றும் திண்டுக்கல் மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

19-05-2026: தமிழகத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களிலும், புதுவை மற்றும் காரைக்கால் பகுதிகளிலும் லேசானது முதல் மிதமான மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.நீலகிரி, தேனி மற்றும் திண்டுக்கல் மாவட்டத்தில் ஓரிரு இடங்களில் இடி மின்னலுடன் கூடிய கன மழை பெய்யக்கூடும்.

இதையும் படிங்க: பெங்களூருவுக்கு வரும் புதிய மெட்ரோ ரயில் சேவை.. பயண நேரம் மற்றும் வழித்தடங்கள் குறித்த முக்கிய அறிவிப்பு!

சென்னை வானிலை நிலவரம்:
சென்னையில் இன்று வானம் ஓரளவு மேகமூட்டத்துடன் காணப்படும். நகரின் ஒருசில பகுதிகளில் லேசான மழை பெய்ய வாய்ப்புள்ளது. அதிகபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 35-36° செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும், குறைந்தபட்ச வெப்பநிலை 26-27° செல்சியஸை ஒட்டியும் இருக்கக்கூடும்.

மீனவர்களுக்கான எச்சரிக்கை:

வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகள்:

அந்தமான் கடல் பகுதிகள், மத்தியகிழக்கு வங்கக்கடலின் சில பகுதிகள் மற்றும் அதனை ஒட்டிய மத்தியமேற்கு வங்கக்கடல் பகுதிகளில் சூறாவளிக்காற்று மணிக்கு 40 முதல் 50 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் இடையிடையே 60 கிலோ மீட்டர் வேகத்திலும் வீசக்கூடும். எனவே மீனவர்கள் இப்பகுதிகளுக்கு செல்ல வேண்டாமென்று அறிவுறுத்தப்படுகிறார்கள்.” இவ்வாறு அதில் தெரிவிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது.

title: Weather Update: ਪੰਜਾਬ ‘ਚ ਪਵੇਗੀ ਭਾਰੀ ਗਰਮੀ: 17 ਮਈ ਤੋਂ ‘ਹੀਟ ਵੇਵ’ ਦਾ ਅਲਰਟ; ਮਾਨਸੂਨ ਨੂੰ ਲੈ ਕੇ ਵੀ ਆਈ ਵੱਡੀ ਅਪਡੇਟ - Desh Click
article_text:
ਚੰਡੀਗੜ੍ਹ: ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਰਹਿ ਰਹੇ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਲਈ ਆਉਣ ਵਾਲੇ ਦਿਨ ਚੁਣੌਤੀਪੂਰਨ ਹੋ ਸਕਦੇ ਹਨ। ਮੌਸਮ ਵਿਭਾਗ ਨੇ ਭਵਿੱਖਬਾਣੀ ਕੀਤੀ ਹੈ ਕਿ ਸੂਬੇ ਵਿੱਚ ਇੱਕ ਵਾਰ ਫਿਰ ‘ਹੀਟ ਵੇਵ’ (ਲੂ) ਦਾ ਪ੍ਰਕੋਪ ਦੇਖਣ ਨੂੰ ਮਿਲੇਗਾ। ਵਿਭਾਗ ਵੱਲੋਂ 17 ਮਈ ਤੋਂ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਤਿੰਨ ਦਿਨਾਂ ਲਈ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਹੀਟ ਵੇਵ ਦਾ ਅਲਰਟ ਜਾਰੀ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਹੈ। ਮਾਹਿਰਾਂ ਮੁਤਾਬਕ ਆਉਣ ਵਾਲੇ ਦਿਨਾਂ ਵਿੱਚ ਤਾਪਮਾਨ 4 ਤੋਂ 5 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਤੱਕ ਵਧ ਸਕਦਾ ਹੈ।
ਬੁੱਧਵਾਰ ਨੂੰ ਮੌਸਮ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਕ ਰਹਿਣ ਕਾਰਨ ਤਾਪਮਾਨ ਵਿੱਚ 2 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਦਾ ਵਾਧਾ ਦਰਜ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ। ਹਾਲਾਂਕਿ, ਦੇਰ ਸ਼ਾਮ ਪੱਛਮੀ ਵਿਘੋਖ (Western Disturbance) ਦੇ ਅਸਰ ਕਾਰਨ ਕਈ ਥਾਵਾਂ ‘ਤੇ ਤੇਜ਼ ਹਨੇਰੀ ਦੇ ਨਾਲ ਮੀਂਹ ਪਿਆ, ਜਿਸ ਨੇ ਅਸਥਾਈ ਤੌਰ ‘ਤੇ ਗਰਮੀ ਤੋਂ ਰਾਹਤ ਦਿੱਤੀ।
ਫਰੀਦਕੋਟ 43.7 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਤਾਪਮਾਨ ਨਾਲ ਸੂਬੇ ਦਾ ਸਭ ਤੋਂ ਗਰਮ ਸ਼ਹਿਰ ਰਿਹਾ। ਪੰਜਾਬ ਦਾ ਔਸਤ ਘੱਟੋ-ਘੱਟ ਤਾਪਮਾਨ ਆਮ ਨਾਲੋਂ 3.1 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਉੱਪਰ (25-28 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਦੇ ਕਰੀਬ) ਚੱਲ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ। ਸਭ ਤੋਂ ਘੱਟ ਤਾਪਮਾਨ ਐੱਸ.ਬੀ.ਐੱਸ. ਨਗਰ ਵਿੱਚ 18 ਡਿਗਰੀ ਦਰਜ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ।
ਮੌਸਮ ਵਿਭਾਗ ਨੇ ਵੀਰਵਾਰ ਲਈ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਦੇ ਕੁਝ ਹਿੱਸਿਆਂ ਵਿੱਚ 40 ਤੋਂ 50 ਕਿਲੋਮੀਟਰ ਦੀ ਰਫ਼ਤਾਰ ਨਾਲ ਤੇਜ਼ ਹਵਾਵਾਂ ਚੱਲਣ ਅਤੇ ਹਲਕੀ ਬਾਰਿਸ਼ ਹੋਣ ਦੀ ਸੰਭਾਵਨਾ ਜਤਾਈ ਹੈ। ਇਸ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਮੌਸਮ ਪੂਰੀ ਤਰ੍ਹਾਂ ਖ਼ੁਸ਼ਕ ਹੋ ਜਾਵੇਗਾ ਅਤੇ ਗਰਮੀ ਵਧਣੀ ਸ਼ੁਰੂ ਹੋ ਜਾਵੇਗੀ।
ਮੌਸਮ ਵਿਭਾਗ ਚੰਡੀਗੜ੍ਹ ਕੇਂਦਰ ਦੇ ਡਾਇਰੈਕਟਰ ਸੁਰਿੰਦਰ ਪਾਲ ਅਨੁਸਾਰ, ਇਸ ਵਾਰ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਾਨਸੂਨ ਆਮ ਨਾਲੋਂ ਘੱਟ ਰਹਿਣ ਦੀ ਸੰਭਾਵਨਾ ਹੈ। ਆਮ ਤੌਰ ‘ਤੇ ਮਾਨਸੂਨ 22 ਤੋਂ 24 ਜੂਨ ਦੇ ਵਿਚਕਾਰ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਦਸਤਕ ਦਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ, ਪਰ ਇਸ ਵਾਰ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਵਿੱਚ ਇਸ ਦੇ ਪਹੁੰਚਣ ਦਾ ਸਹੀ ਸਮਾਂ ਕੇਰਲ ਵਿੱਚ ਮਾਨਸੂਨ ਦੀ ਐਂਟਰੀ ਤੋਂ ਬਾਅਦ ਹੀ ਸਪੱਸ਼ਟ ਹੋ ਸਕੇਗਾ।

title: ચોમાસાના આગમનના સંકેત વચ્ચે 17 રાજ્યમાં ભારે વરસાદનું એલર્ટ, ગુજરાતમાં હીટવેવની સ્થિતિ - Mumbai Samachar
article_text:
નવી દિલ્હીઃ દેશમાં મોસમનો બેવડો મિજાજ જોવા મળી રહ્યો છે. ક્યાંક ભીષણ ગરમીથી લોકો પરેશાન છે તો ક્યાંક આંધી, વરસાદે કહેર મચાવ્યો છે. હવામાન વિભાગ મુજબ 16 મેની આસપાસ દક્ષિણ-પશ્ચિમ ચોમાસું આંદામાન સાગર અને દક્ષિણ બંગાળની ખાડીના કેટલાક ભાગોમાં આગળ વધી શકે છે. આ સાથે જ, આ અઠવાડિયા દરમિયાન ઉત્તર-પશ્ચિમ, પશ્ચિમ અને મધ્ય ભારતના કેટલાક ભાગોમાં આગામી કેટલાક દિવસો સુધી ભીષણ હીટવેવની સ્થિતિ યથાવત રહેવાની શક્યતા છે. આ દરમિયાન, લોકોને બપોરના સમયે તડકામાં નીકળવાનું ટાળવા અને પૂરતા પ્રમાણમાં પાણી પીવાની સલાહ આપવામાં આવી છે. ઉત્તર-પૂર્વ ભારતમાં 13 થી 19 મે દરમિયાન ભારેથી અતિભારે વરસાદની આગાહી કરવામાં આવી છે. જ્યારે પુડુચેરી, કરાઈકલ, કેરળ, તમિલનાડુ, અને દક્ષિણ  કર્ણાટકમાં 13 થી 17 મે વચ્ચે ઘણા સ્થળોએ ભારે વરસાદની આગાહી વ્યક્ત કરવામાં આવી છે.
ગુજરાતમાં કેવું રહેશે હવામાન
હવામાન વિભાગની આગાહી પ્રમાણે, ગુજરાતમાં આજે કચ્છ અને સુરેન્દ્રનગરમાં ઓરેન્જ એલર્ટ આપવામાં આવ્યું છે. અહીં આગ ઓકતી ગરમી પડવાની આગાહી છે. આ સાથે દ્વારકા, રાજકોટ, અમરેલી, બોટાદ, અમદાવાદ, વડોદરા, ગાંધીનગર, પાટણ, બનાસકાંઠામાં યલો એલર્ટ સાથે હીટવેવની આગાહી આપવામાં આવી છે. ગુજરાતના અનેક વિસ્તારોમાં આગામી બે દિવસ 41થી 45 ડિગ્રી તાપમાન રહેવાનું અનુમાન છે. 48 કલાક બાદ પવનની દિશા બદલાઈ શકે છે. 48 કલાક બાદ ધીમે ધીમે બેથી ચાર ડિગ્રી તાપમાન ઓછું થઈ શકે છે.
આ વર્ષે ચોમાસાની શરૂઆત પહેલાં જ પૂર્વ-ચોમાસાની હવામાન પ્રવૃત્તિ જોવા મળી રહી છે. દક્ષિણ બંગાળની ખાડી અને આંદામાન અને નિકોબાર ક્ષેત્રમાં ચોમાસાની પ્રવૃત્તિના પ્રારંભિક સંકેતોએ સમગ્ર દક્ષિણ ભારતમાં હવામાન પરિસ્થિતિઓમાં ઝડપથી ફેરફાર કર્યા છે. કેરળ, તમિલનાડુ અને કર્ણાટકના કેટલાક ભાગોમાં ભારે વરસાદની ચેતવણી જારી કરવામાં આવી છે. દરમિયાન, ઉત્તરપૂર્વ ભારતમાં, સતત વાદળછાયું વાતાવરણ અને વરસાદે ઉનાળાની ગરમીની તીવ્રતાને ઓછી કરવામાં મદદ કરી છે. તેનાથી વિપરીત, રાજસ્થાન, ગુજરાત અને મધ્યપ્રદેશના કેટલાક પ્રદેશોમાં હીટવેવની સ્થિતિ યથાવત છે.
હવામાનશાસ્ત્રીઓ માને છે કે, બંગાળની ખાડી અને અરબી સમુદ્રની આસપાસ વિકસિત હવામાન પ્રણાલીઓ સમગ્ર ઉત્તર ભારતની હવામાન પેટર્નને પ્રભાવિત કરી રહી છે. પરિણામે, જ્યારે લોકો ભીષણ ગરમીથી થોડી રાહતની અપેક્ષા રાખી શકે છે, ત્યારે વીજળી, કરા પડવા અને અચાનક હવામાન બગડવાનું જોખમ એક સાથે વધ્યું છે.
બંગાળની ખાડીમાં હવાનું હળવું દબાણ સક્રિય થયું છે. જેના પગલે માછીમારોને 14 થી 18 મે દરમિયાન દક્ષિણ-પશ્ચિમ બંગાળની ખાડી, આંદામાન સમુદ્ર અને મધ્ય બંગાળની ખાડીમાં ન જવાની સલાહ આપવામાં આવી છે. આ વિસ્તારોમાં 40-50 કિમીથી લઈને 60 કિમીની ઝડપે પવન ફૂંકાઈ શકે છે.
દિલ્હીમાં આજે હળવો છૂટોછવાયો વરસાદ પડી શકે છે. ઉત્તર પ્રદેશના અનેક જિલ્લામાં ગાજવીજ સાથે હળવાથી મધ્યમ વરસાદની આગાહી છે. આ દરમિયાન 30 થી 60 કિમી પ્રતિ કલાકની ઝડપે પવન ફૂંકાઈ શકે છે.  બિહારમાં 14 થી 17 મે સુધી હળવો વરસાદ અને આંધી-તોફાન રહેશે. હિમાચલ પ્રદેસમાં 15 અને 16 મેના રોજ વરસાદ અને ઊંચાણવાળા વિસ્તારમાં બરફ વર્ષા થઈ શકે છે.
રાજસ્થાનમાં ભીષણ ગરમી અને લૂનો પ્રકોપ જોવા મળશે. હવામાન વિભાગે પશ્ચિમ રાજસ્થાનમાં 15 મે સુધી તથા અન્ય વિસ્તારમાં 18 મે સુધી હીટવેવનું એલર્ટ જાહેર કર્યું છે. પંજાબમાં બેવડું વાતાવરણ જોવા મળશે. અનેક વિસ્તારમાં ગાજવીજ સાથે હળવો વરસાદ અને ભારે પવન ફૂંકાશે. જ્યારે બોર્ડર વિસ્તારમાં આંધીની અસર જોવા મળશે.
